page: Google Cloud Platform n1-highmem-8 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the N1 machine family. It features 8 vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores of an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.3 GHz with hyper-threading enabled. The instance is equipped with 52.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 6.5 GB per core. It utilizes the x86_64 architecture and includes a 45 MB L3 cache. This instance does not include bundled local storage or GPUs, and provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks indicate a moderate capability profile. The single-core CPU performance is weak, falling in the bottom 25% on stress-ng, while multi-core performance reaches average levels. Memory bandwidth tests for both cached small blocks and uncached large blocks show average performance, with read-only large block speeds at 44.59 GB/sec and write speeds at 34.47 GB/sec. Computational tasks such as gzip compression and bzip3 decompression show poor single-threaded performance. However, database operations and Redis SET operations perform at average levels, with Redis achieving 5.73 million operations per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the n1-highmem-8 offers a high memory density relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require top-tier CPU performance. The tradeoff involves lower single-core processing speeds and lack of local storage in exchange for a larger memory footprint. This server is best suited for database operations, in-memory caching systems like Redis, and moderate-scale data processing tasks where memory capacity is prioritized over raw compute power.
description: Google Cloud Platform n1-highmem-8 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance featuring 8 vCPUs and 52.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.3 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture with 4 physical cores and hyper-threading, it provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 6.5 GB per core. The instance does not include local storage or GPUs. Benchmark data shows average multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth, alongside average database and Redis SET operation speeds, despite weak single-core performance. This configuration is qualitatively cost-effective for memory-intensive workloads that do not demand high single-threaded CPU speeds. It is well-suited for database hosting, in-memory caching, and general-purpose workloads requiring a larger memory footprint.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-highmem-8 is an x86_64 memory-optimized instance with 8 vCPUs, 52 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, offering balanced multi-core and database performance for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-highmem-8 is an x86_64 memory-optimized instance with 8 vCPUs, 52 GB RAM, and average database performance.
tagline: An x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine featuring eight vCPUs and fifty-two gigabytes of RAM for memory-intensive database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 8 vCPUs and 52.0 GB of RAM providing 6.5 GB per core
Intel Xeon 2.3 GHz processor with 45 MB L3 cache
Average performance in database and Redis SET operations
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Suited for in-memory caching and database workloads
